programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Extended Hours: AM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care before the start of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp.Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Bronze Star
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: Pilates

programs.name: Ranger Patrol
programs.description: The Ranger Patrol program focuses on performance with a 200-metre timed swim and 100-metre fitness medley. It also provides instruction on assessing unconscious victims, treatment for shock and obstructed airway procedures.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Rookie Patrol.

programs.name: Ranger Patrol
programs.description: The Ranger Patrol program focuses on performance with a 200-metre timed swim and 100-metre fitness medley. It also provides instruction on assessing unconscious victims, treatment for shock and obstructed airway procedures.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Rookie Patrol.

programs.name: Rookie Patrol
programs.description: The Rookie Patrol program focuses on performance with a 100-metre timed swim and 350-metre workouts. It also provides instruction on assessing conscious victims, treatment of bleeding, and contacting Emergency Medical Services (EMS).Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: SPLASH Swim Team
programs.description: Expand your swimming skills by practicing your strokes in a team-based environment and showcase your skills during competitive swim meets. Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths. IMPORTANT: The City of Toronto is dedicated to ensuring the safety of all individuals who participate in its activities and services. This program requires the review of Ontario's Concussion Awareness Resources and the City of Toronto's Concussion Code of Conduct. A signature of acknowledgment is required for participants under the age of 26, or parents/caregivers of participants under the age of 18, on the first day of program participation.Visit www.toronto.ca/concussionsafety for more information on concussion safety in our sports and recreation programs.

programs.name: Star Patrol
programs.description: The Star Patrol program focuses on performance with a 300-metre timed swim and 600-metre workouts. It also provides instruction on treatment of bone and joint injuries as well as responding to respiratory emergencies including asthma and allergic reactions.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Ranger Patrol.

programs.name: Step
programs.description: Step up your fitness routine with this fun cardio workout. Here movement is centered around and performed on a small platform or "Step" to help increase intensity. With a focus on using the whole body, you will build up a sweat while improving your balance, strength and agility.

programs.name: Strength and Conditioning
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve you overall endurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Participants learn to organize, plan, teach and evaluate swimming, lifesaving and first aid skills. Discover and develop skills in the areas of class management, teaching, evaluation, leadership, communication and administration. With the successful completion of this course, candidates will be qualified to instruct the Toronto Learn to Swim program, the Lifesaving Society Swim for Life program, Emergency First Aid with CPR-B, Rookie, Ranger and Star Patrol as well as Bronze Star, Medallion and Cross.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old and Bronze Cross (current or expired).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Deep
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? programs.name: Workshop: Beaded Spiders
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? A beaded spider project is a beginner-friendly small projects. It's made using wire, different beads your creation can be used as a brooch, ornament or table decoration

programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Deep
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? With the support of belts and noodles, this activity focuses on combining movement and staying afloat in deep water. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to perform a facility swim test.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Mind Body
programs.description: Combine the calm of an aquatic environment with mindful movements. Focusing on balance and breath, elements from Pilates, tai chi and yoga will be incorporated into this workout.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Gymnastics
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. In this camp, you will learn and practice basic skills such as front roll progressions, handstands, and cartwheels, as well as floor skills like leaps jumps and turns.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your gymnastics skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Soccer
programs.description: Calling all soccer fans! Through practice and play, you will build on skills like kicking, passing and heading while learning the rules of the game an practicing respectful play.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your soccer skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics, or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Swim Ultra
programs.description: Enhance and develop your swim skills at camp.  Every day, you will take part in small group lessons for 30 minutes at a City of Toronto pool. In addition to focusing on developing and fine-tuning your swim skills, you will also enjoy games, arts and sports. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Visual Arts
programs.description: In this camp you are the artist. You will explore a wide variety of art mediums and learn the secrets of painting, sketching, drawing and more. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your artist skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Cardio Dance: Afro and Soca Fusion
programs.description: Afro and Soca fusion beats and rhythms inspire this high-intensity cardio workout. Using fast steps and full-body movements like squats, jumps and body waves, you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Cardio: High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Cardio: Kickboxing
programs.description: Calling all fitness warriors. This high-intensity cardio workout that incorporates non-contact punches, kicks and athletic moves will help you improve your strength, balance and coordination.

programs.name: Crafty Creations
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. As you continue to practice and develop skills, you will work towards performing a routine at the end of the session.

programs.name: Dance: Ballroom
programs.description: Get ready to glide across the dance floor with ease. Learn basic steps, postures and holds of various dance styles like the waltz, foxtrot, tango, rumba and quick step through various exercises and engaging choreography.Partners are not required as dancers will be partnered in this class as needed.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.name: Garden and Nature with Caregiver
programs.description: Young people who love nature will enjoy this combination of gardening and outdoor exploration. programs.location: Toronto Island Park - Franklin Children's Gardn

programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.name: Garden and Nature with Caregiver
programs.description: Young people who love nature will enjoy this combination of gardening and outdoor exploration. programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: SPLASH Swim Team
programs.description: Expand your swimming skills by practicing your strokes in a team-based environment and showcase your skills during competitive swim meets. Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths. IMPORTANT: The City of Toronto is dedicated to ensuring the safety of all individuals who participate in its activities and services. This program requires the review of Ontario's Concussion Awareness Resources and the City of Toronto's Concussion Code of Conduct. A signature of acknowledgment is required for participants under the age of 26, or parents/caregivers of participants under the age of 18, on the first day of program participation.Visit www.toronto.ca/concussionsafety for more information on concussion safety in our sports and recreation programs.

programs.name: Step
programs.description: Step up your fitness routine with this fun cardio workout. Here movement is centered around and performed on a small platform or "Step" to help increase intensity. With a focus on using the whole body, you will build up a sweat while improving your balance, strength and agility.

programs.name: Step
programs.description: Step up your fitness routine with this fun cardio workout. Here movement is centered around and performed on a small platform or "Step" to help increase intensity. With a focus on using the whole body, you will build up a sweat while improving your balance, strength and agility.

programs.name: Swim Fit
programs.description: The course name says it all—Swim Fit. In this activity, you’ll not only get instruction on technique and stroke improvement, but you’ll also get the opportunity to perform interval drills to increase your swimming performance and endurance.Prerequisite: Must be able to swim two continuous lengths of the pool or 50 metres.

programs.name: Tai Chi
programs.description: Embrace the body and mind through the practice of tai chi . Learn how to pair gentle movements that you will perform in a sequence with controlled breathing, to improve your flexibility, coordination, strength and mental concentration.

programs.name: Tai Chi
programs.description: Embrace the body and mind through the practice of tai chi . Learn how to pair gentle movements that you will perform in a sequence with controlled breathing, to improve your flexibility, coordination, strength and mental concentration.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational pickleball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.This is a Subscriber program; there is no prorated charge for withdrawing/registering once the program has started.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Qigong
programs.description: Take part in a series of mind and body exercises to improve your flexibility, strength and posture. With focused intention and relaxed movement, you will learn how to integrate breathing and meditation into your wellness routine.

programs.name: Ranger Patrol
programs.description: The Ranger Patrol program focuses on performance with a 200-metre timed swim and 100-metre fitness medley. It also provides instruction on assessing unconscious victims, treatment for shock and obstructed airway procedures.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Rookie Patrol.

programs.name: Rookie Patrol
programs.description: The Rookie Patrol program focuses on performance with a 100-metre timed swim and 350-metre workouts. It also provides instruction on assessing conscious victims, treatment of bleeding, and contacting Emergency Medical Services (EMS).Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Soccer
programs.description: Get moving with non-stop soccer play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, drop-in soccer offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Soccer

A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this soccer activity, you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: SPLASH Swim Team
programs.description: Expand your swimming skills by practicing your strokes in a team-based environment and showcase your skills during competitive swim meets. Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths. IMPORTANT: The City of Toronto is dedicated to ensuring the safety of all individuals who participate in its activities and services. This program requires the review of Ontario's Concussion Awareness Resources and the City of Toronto's Concussion Code of Conduct. A signature of acknowledgment is required for participants under the age of 26, or parents/caregivers of participants under the age of 18, on the first day of program participation.Visit www.toronto.ca/concussionsafety for more information on concussion safety in our sports and recreation programs.

programs.name: Star Patrol
programs.description: The Star Patrol program focuses on performance with a 300-metre timed swim and 600-metre workouts. It also provides instruction on treatment of bone and joint injuries as well as responding to respiratory emergencies including asthma and allergic reactions.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Ranger Patrol.

programs.name: Step
programs.description: Step up your fitness routine with this fun cardio workout. Here movement is centered around and performed on a small platform or "Step" to help increase intensity. With a focus on using the whole body, you will build up a sweat while improving your balance, strength and agility.

programs.name: Strength and Conditioning
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve your overall endurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Participants learn to organize, plan, teach and evaluate swimming, lifesaving and first aid skills. Discover and develop skills in the areas of class management, teaching, evaluation, leadership, communication and administration. With the successful completion of this course, candidates will be qualified to instruct the Toronto Learn to Swim program, the Lifesaving Society Swim for Life program, Emergency First Aid with CPR-B, Rookie, Ranger and Star Patrol as well as Bronze Star, Medallion and Cross.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old and Bronze Cross (current or expired).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Swim Clinic: Waterfront Junior Lifesaving Sport
programs.description:
This Program will introduce participants to waterfront lifesaving through open water swimming, paddle boarding, and dry land activities. This program meets at Summerville outdoor pool and takes place on woodbine beach. Participants should be prepared to swim in lake Ontario.

Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths.
programs.location: Meet at south side of Summerville, course takes place on the beach and in the lake

Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Basketball
programs.description: Hit the court for non-stop basketball play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, drop-in basketball offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.

programs.name: Bronze Cross & Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Intermediate First Aid CPR-C & AED certification (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24) provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and one of the following first aid certifications (current or expired) from an approved provider: Emergency First Aid with CPR-B or Basic First Aid with CPR-C & AED or Standard First Aid with CPR-C or Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion and current Canadian First Aid ManualFor information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Star
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Tennis
programs.description: Calling all tennis fans! Through practice and play, you will build on tennis-specific conditioning and coordination while learning game rules and developing fundamentals including serving, forehand and backhand. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your tennis skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp. Equipment: Campers must bring their own tennis racquet.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Visual Arts
programs.description: In this camp you are the artist. You will explore a wide variety of art mediums and learn the secrets of painting, sketching, drawing and more.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your artist skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Cardio Dance
programs.description: Cardio dance is a dynamic and exciting aerobic workout that anyone can enjoy. With a focus on various culturally inspired music and dance movements and a goal of lifting your heart rate you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Cardio: High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise?  With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Cardio: High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise?  With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Challenge Swim: Gold
programs.description: Take on the challenge of improving your fitness and endurance. In this third and final level of the Challenge Swim program you will learn relay starts while continuing to work on interval training and swimming drills. Work towards a 500-metre continuous swim in a non-competitive environment, where you will aim to improve your swim times and skills.Prerequisite: Successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7 or Challenge Silver.

programs.name: Chinese Fan Painting
programs.description: Participants will explore the rich cultural history of Chinese fan painting while learning foundational brush techniques, composition and symbolism. Guided step-by-step, you'll practice using ink on delicare surfaces, introducing the basics of brush control, ink flow and the balance of this centuries-old practice. By the end of the session, you'll leave with your own hand-painted fan - a unique piece that blends artistry, culture and personal expression. This workshop requires an additional $40 material fee (cash only), payable to the instructor for inks and fan. A materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: Crafty Creations with Caregiver
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. As you continue to practice and develop skills, you will work towards performing a routine at the end of the session.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: K-Pop
programs.description: Korean pop, or K-pop, is a music genre that has inspired its own dance style, and this is your chance to learn it. Blending classic dance moves from jazz and hip-hop with Korean pop sounds, you will learn basic routines through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Intermediate (Bike Handling Skills)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is a great way to take your cycling to the next level.
